purpose: Design and test coverage baseline for supported user workflows
assumptions: Workflows cover typical user goals rather than every CLI flag.
CLI, Web UI, and Claude Code skills should remain functionally aligned where capabilities overlap.
LLM-dependent workflows must also define graceful behavior when models, gateway config, API keys, or slide images are unavailable.

workflows.name: Initial setup and configuration
workflows.goal: Install AIPPT, initialize model settings, and configure optional LLM gateway access.
preconditions: optional provider or gateway API credentials
steps: Create and activate virtual environment.
Install Python dependencies.
Initialize models.yaml from bundled example.
Set provider API keys or configure gateway.yaml.
Optionally set default template and per-operation model defaults.
expected_outcomes: CLI help works.
Model defaults can be listed and changed.
Gateway-routed LLM calls can be made when credentials are present.
LLM features fail clearly when configuration is missing.

workflows.name: Author markdown outline and generate deck from CLI
workflows.goal: Create a PowerPoint deck from a markdown outline and template.
entry_points: python aippt.py create outline.md template.pptx output.pptx
steps: Write outline using simple or hierarchical markdown structure.
Optionally add frontmatter for audience, goal, and tone.
Optionally add LAYOUT and IMAGE directives.
Run create command.
Open generated PPTX and inspect slide count, layouts, text, images, and notes.
expected_outcomes: PPTX is created.
Sections and slide titles match the outline.
Supported layouts render correctly.
Missing images or invalid layouts warn and fall back without crashing.

workflows.name: Generate enhanced deck with AI
workflows.goal: Use an LLM to improve layout, narrative flow, slide titles, notes, and visual planning during deck creation.
entry_points: Web UI Create Deck from Outline
steps: Choose target audience.
Run deck creation with enhance enabled.
Optionally inspect the narrative plan.
Generate the deck.
Review enhanced titles, content structure, notes, and visual choices.
expected_outcomes: Deck preserves outline intent while improving presentation quality.
Audience selection changes language depth appropriately.
Author-specified LAYOUT directives override LLM layout suggestions.
Progress is visible in the web UI during generation.

workflows.name: Create outline from source material using skill workflow
workflows.goal: Turn documents, repos, web pages, directories, or freeform topics into an AIPPT-compatible outline.
steps: Provide source path, URL, repo, directory, or topic.
Gather and analyze source content.
Define audience, goal, and tone.
Generate and iterate on outline.
Optionally enrich visual slides with screenshots or TODO placeholders.
expected_outcomes: Markdown outline is saved under outlines/.
Outline follows AIPPT format conventions.
Visual needs are explicitly represented with IMAGE directives or TODOs.
User can pass the result to /create-deck or aippt create.

workflows.name: Generate slides-as-code deck using skill workflow
workflows.goal: Create a polished PPTX and reproducible build script from an outline.
preconditions: Node.js for pptxgenjs or Python environment for python-pptx
steps: Select an outline.
Choose pptxgenjs for creative themed decks or python-pptx for strict template use.
Choose theme or corporate template.
Generate build script.
Run build script to create PPTX.
expected_outcomes: PPTX is created in output/.
Build script is saved as the source of truth.
Theme tokens, layouts, masters, and section dividers are applied where supported.
Dense slides are identifiable for later review.

workflows.name: Review and improve generated deck
workflows.goal: Perform visual QA, collect feedback, generate notes, and improve slides after deck creation.
steps: Render or provide slide images.
Review slides for visual quality, overflow, contrast, alignment, and content issues.
Run AI feedback, notes, tags, or improvement suggestions.
Optionally apply improvements to selected slides.
Preserve review or edit metadata where supported.
expected_outcomes: Reviewer receives actionable slide feedback.
Speaker notes can be generated.
Improvement dry-runs show proposed changes before writing.
Applied improvements update the deck or chosen output file.

workflows.name: Reverse existing PowerPoint to markdown outline
workflows.goal: Convert a PPTX into a reusable markdown outline for regeneration or editing.
preconditions: optional slide images and LLM model for enhanced reverse
steps: Run reverse conversion.
Choose whether to include, exclude, or strip speaker notes.
Optionally run enhanced multimodal reverse with slide images.
Review generated markdown outline.
expected_outcomes: Markdown outline is generated.
Slide titles and bullet hierarchy are preserved as much as possible.
Speaker notes do not leak into slide body.
Enhanced mode produces higher-quality outline language when configured.

workflows.name: Ingest and catalog presentation library
workflows.goal: Add existing decks and slide thumbnails to the searchable SQLite catalog.
preconditions: PowerPoint image export support or existing slide images
steps: Export slide images or let ingest attempt export.
Catalog deck metadata, slide text, notes, hashes, image paths, and source tracking.
Optionally generate tags during ingest.
Verify deck appears in catalog.
expected_outcomes: Deck and slides are stored in SQLite.
Images are linked to slides.
Re-cataloging detects existing content and updates metadata safely.
Uploaded decks are downloadable from the web UI.

workflows.name: Browse, search, and tag slide library
workflows.goal: Find reusable slides by deck, title, section, or tags and curate tags manually.
steps: Open deck list.
Browse a deck's slide grid.
Open slide detail modal.
Search by title or tags.
Add or remove manual tags.
expected_outcomes: Deck list and slide grid display expected metadata.
Search results match title and tag filters.
Manual tags persist in the database.
Tag sidebar filters library content.

workflows.name: Manage taxonomy and model defaults
workflows.goal: Control allowed tags and default LLM models per operation.
steps: Add, remove, rename, import, or export taxonomy tags.
Set default models for enhance, feedback, notes, tags, image, reverse, and improve operations.
Set default template path.
Validate settings are persisted.
expected_outcomes: Taxonomy changes affect future taxonomy-constrained tagging.
Renaming taxonomy tags updates slide associations.
Invalid model defaults are rejected.
Settings survive reloads.

workflows.name: Run AI analysis and tagging on cataloged slides
workflows.goal: Generate design feedback, speaker notes, improvement suggestions, or tags for slides.
preconditions: slide images for visual analysis unless text-only fallback is acceptable
steps: Select analysis mode: feedback, notes, tags, or improvements.
Choose model or rely on defaults.
Provide images directory or use cataloged image paths.
Run analysis.
Review console or web results.
expected_outcomes: Feedback and improvements are displayed.
Notes can be written to PPTX or stored in DB.
Tags are added as AI or taxonomy source.
Text-only fallback works when images are missing for CLI analysis.

workflows.name: Edit speaker notes and write back to deck
workflows.goal: Manage speaker notes in the catalog and write them back into the source PPTX.
steps: Open slide detail.
Edit speaker notes manually or generate suggested notes.
Save notes to database.
Review notes edit history.
Write catalog notes back to the original PPTX with backup.
expected_outcomes: Notes edits persist in the database.
Edit history records old value, new value, source, and timestamp.
Write-back creates a backup.
Slides with conflicts or missing source are reported clearly.

workflows.name: Export, remix, and reuse slides
workflows.goal: Export catalog metadata or assemble a new deck from selected existing slides.
steps: Search for desired slides.
Export search results as a remix manifest.
Optionally edit manifest manually.
Run remix to assemble a new PPTX.
Export full or deck-specific catalog metadata to CSV.
expected_outcomes: CSV contains expected slide metadata.
Manifest references correct source decks and positions.
Remixed deck opens and contains selected slides in order.
Missing manifests or source files produce clear errors.

workflows.name: Operate library in view-only or portable mode
workflows.goal: Run AIPPT as a browse-only slide library and keep catalog paths portable.
steps: Launch web UI in view-only mode or without LLM config.
Browse decks, slides, tags, search, downloads, and CSV export.
Verify LLM endpoints are disabled.
Migrate absolute paths to relative paths when moving the library.
Backup and restore catalog assets when needed.
expected_outcomes: Non-LLM browsing features work.
LLM actions are disabled with clear messages.
Relative paths resolve from project root.
Library can move across machines or containers.

workflows.name: Use corporate template, themes, and metadata workflows
workflows.goal: Generate branded decks, merge generated content into a corporate template, and inspect embedded AI metadata.
preconditions: corporate template or themed build script
steps: Generate deck using selected theme or template.
Optionally merge multiple section PPTX files.
Merge generated deck into corporate template.
Inspect AI metadata embedded in speaker notes.
Validate slide masters, chrome, theme tokens, and layout mapping.
expected_outcomes: Final deck uses expected corporate styling.
Merged sections preserve intended order.
Dry-run layout mapping is inspectable.
Metadata can be extracted as JSON.

workflows.name: Generate or attach visual assets through directives and MCP
workflows.goal: Add diagrams, screenshots, or generated images to slides through IMAGE directives or MCP-backed image generation.
preconditions: local image files or MCP image server config
steps: Add IMAGE directives to slides.
Use diagram or two-column layouts when appropriate.
Configure MCP server if generating images.
Run deck generation.
Verify visuals are placed correctly and text moves to notes when required.
expected_outcomes: Existing images are embedded.
Missing images warn without failing the whole deck.
MCP image generation can discover configured tools.
Diagram slides allocate full content area appropriately.
